description: Twin-Strep-tag: two tandem Strep-tag II (WSHPQFEK) units joined by a GGGSGGGSGGS linker, conferring bivalent high-avidity binding to Strep-Tactin/Strep-Tactin XT for one-step affinity purification and detection.

references.authors: Schmidt TGM, Batz L, Bonet L, Carl U, Holzapfel G, Kiem K, Matulewicz K, Niermeier D, Schuchardt I, Stanar K
references.title: Development of the Twin-Strep-tag and its application for purification of recombinant proteins from cell culture supernatants

references.authors: Schmidt TGM, Koepke J, Frank R, Skerra A
references.title: Molecular interaction between the Strep-tag affinity peptide and its cognate target, streptavidin

references.authors: Eichinger A, Schmidt TGM, Skerra A
references.title: The Role of Changing Loop Conformations in Streptavidin Versions Engineered for High-affinity Binding of the Strep-tag II Peptide
The Strep-tag system for one-step purification and high-affinity detection or capturing of proteins
The Strep-tag system for one-step affinity purification of proteins from mammalian cell culture

label: Twin_Strep_tag
note: Tandem affinity tag: two Strep-tag II (WSHPQFEK) units connected by a GGGSGGGSGGS flexible linker, with an N-terminal Ser-Ala. Bivalency lowers the off-rate from Strep-Tactin/Strep-Tactin XT, giving picomolar-range avidity and enabling near-irreversible capture with mild competitive (biotin/desthiobiotin) elution. Sequence confirmed 100% over 30 aa against PDB 6SOS chain R.

provenance.sequence_source: PDB 6SOS chain R (Schmidt, Eichinger, Schneider, Bonet, Carl, Karthaus, Theobald, Skerra; J.Mol.Biol. 2021, PMID 33639211, DOI 10.1016/j.jmb.2021.166893), "Twin-Strep-tag peptide", synthetic construct (taxon:32630), sequence SAWSHPQFEKGGGSGGGSGGSAWSHPQFEK, 100% identity over 30 aa; independently fetched via RCSB FASTA API (https://www.rcsb.org/fasta/entry/6SOS/display) and compared byte-for-byte to the canonical IBA Twin-Strep-tag sequence.

functional_claims.label: Bivalent tandem display of two Strep-tag II units connected by a GGGSGGGSGGS linker lowers the dissociation rate from engineered streptavidin, giving an avidity (multivalent) binding mode.

source.quote: Development of the Twin-Strep-tag and its application for purification of recombinant proteins from cell culture supernatants

functional_claims.label: Functions as a portable affinity/detection tag independent of expression host; demonstrated for proteins secreted into mammalian cell-culture supernatants and broadly used across prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems.

source.quote: application for purification of recombinant proteins from cell culture supernatants

functional_claims.label: Bivalent binding to engineered streptavidin (Strep-Tactin/Strep-Tactin XT) gives near-irreversible, picomolar-range capture, far tighter than a single Strep-tag II (micromolar to Strep-Tactin).

source.quote: structural basis of high-affinity bivalent binding of the Twin-Strep-tag to engineered streptavidin (Strep-Tactin XT)

functional_claims.label: Distinct from a patent-filing variant of identical length (30 aa) that encodes SAWSHPQFEKGGGSGGGSGGGSWSHPQFEK (GGGS x3 linker, no Ser-Ala before the second unit). This record is the canonical product sequence SAWSHPQFEKGGGSGGGSGGSAWSHPQFEK (GGGSGGGSGGS linker).

source.quote: Twin-Strep-tag peptide chain R, sequence SAWSHPQFEKGGGSGGGSGGSAWSHPQFEK
